question.text: Delivery versus payment (DVP) in commodity settlement means:

answer.text: Delivery of the commodity happens simultaneously with payment — ensuring neither party bears settlement risk
Delivery happens first, payment follows after 30 days
Payment is made first, delivery follows in the next month
Either delivery or payment, but not both

explanation.text: **The Logic:** DVP ensures simultaneous exchange of commodity and funds — the seller delivers goods and receives payment at the same time, eliminating settlement risk. **The Trap:** There's no 30-day gap. Payment and delivery are simultaneous, not sequential. Both happen, not either/or. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VI covers DVP in securities settlement. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** DVP through the clearing corporation is the safest settlement method — it ensures neither party is exposed to the risk of delivering without receiving consideration.
